name: Launchpad Flow
description: Guided sequence that shows how a builder moves from selecting a Decantr blueprint to initializing, refreshing, checking, and iterating with AI-ready context.

steps.description: Four-step horizontal or stacked flow: Choose, Initialize, Refresh, Verify. Each step carries a compact command or artifact reference.

slots.step-command: Optional CLI command or file artifact.
slots.step-proof: Short note about what changes after this step.

visual_brief: A crisp operational flow that makes Decantr feel immediately usable. The sequence should look like a product workflow, not a tutorial wall. Use clear step labels, small command blocks, and simple connective rhythm so users understand the path from registry choice to verified project health.

responsive.mobile: Stack steps vertically with compact command blocks and visible step numbers.
responsive.desktop: Four-column flow with subtle connectors or aligned step markers.

layout_hints.step_count: Use three to five steps. If more detail is needed, move it into an expandable or docs link outside the core flow.
layout_hints.commands: Commands should be short and copyable. Avoid large code blocks inside this pattern.
layout_hints.outcomes: Every step should state the artifact or confidence it creates: essence, context, health report, or remediation prompt.

motion.micro: Step cards can highlight in sequence on hover or focus.
motion.transitions: Initial reveal may stagger from first step to last over 200-320ms.

keyboard: Copyable commands and links should be focusable without trapping users in the flow.
announcements: Expose each step number, title, command, and outcome in source order.
accessibility.focus_management: Focused steps should show the same state as hover and should not depend on connector visuals.
